---
title: "How to cache your SSH key passphrase with `ssh-agent`"
subtitle: "Note#20"
id: "20"
date: 2022-11-24
date-modified: 2026-04-25
date-format: iso
categories: [notes]
spotlight: ""
---
## For the current shell

```bash
eval "$(ssh-agent)"
ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
```

Enter your passphrase when prompted. It is cached for the rest of the shell session.

## Across shells with `keychain`

[`keychain`](https://github.com/danielrobbins/keychain) starts or reuses a long-running `ssh-agent` and loads your keys.

Add this to `~/.bashrc` or `~/.zshrc`:

```bash
if command -v keychain &>/dev/null; then
    eval "$(keychain --eval --quiet id_ed25519)"
fi
```

## List loaded keys

```bash
ssh-add -l
```

## Related Notes

* [Note#24](/notes/24/)
* [Note#88](/notes/88/)
